title: Lavauxia triloba

It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lavauxia triloba include sesquiterpenoid lactones and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lavauxia triloba is commonly prepared in herbal form, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use and efficacy are not extensively documented in Western medicine.
